label: Tariff Rate on China (2025–2026)
source: Office of the US Trade Representative
deltaNote: From 25% baseline; SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs Feb 2026; China rate remains via Section 301

label: Average US Tariff Rate (Feb 2026)
contestNote: Down from 27% peak after Supreme Court struck IEEPA tariffs; 10% universal tariff now in place via Section 122 for 150 days
deltaNote: Highest level in modern era; SCOTUS ruling forced restructuring of tariff regime

label: SW Border Encounters (2025)
deltaNote: Compared to Dec 2023 peak of ~302,000/month; 605,000+ deported, 1.9M self-deported since Jan 2025

label: Jan. 6 Pardons Issued
contestNote: Critics say blanket pardons include violent rioters; supporters call it correcting prosecutorial overreach

label: Executive Orders (2nd Term)
deltaNote: Pharma tariff EO (100% on patented drugs) and metal tariff EO (flat 50%) signed Apr 2; College Sports EO signed Apr 3; as of April 6, 2026

label: Federal Workforce Reduction (2025)
contestNote: Federal workforce fell ~12% from Sept 2024 to Jan 2026 (2.31M to 2.04M); OPM now signaling some rehiring needed; costs ~$135B per Partnership for Public Service

label: US National Debt
deltaNote: Grew from ~$20T when Trump first took office in 2017; OBBBA adds est. $4.1T more over 10 years (CBO)

label: DOGE Claimed Savings (1-Year Mark)
contestNote: Cato Institute found 'no noticeable effect on trajectory of spending'; federal expenditures rose year-over-year per Treasury; mass firings/rehiring estimated to cost $135B; IRS projects $500B in lost tax revenue; GAO disputes figures

label: US Unemployment Rate (Mar 2026)
deltaNote: March 2026 NFP: +178,000 jobs (est. 59,000), unemployment fell to 4.3% from 4.4% in Feb; wage growth +3.5% y/y (lowest since May 2021); health care led gains (+76K); reported Good Friday Apr 3 while markets were closed

label: Trump Approval Rating (Apr 19, 2026)
contestNote: CNN: 35% approve overall, economy approval career-low 31%; Silver Bulletin aggregate: 37% approve, net -16.6; Quinnipiac: 38% approve, 56% disapprove; two-thirds of Americans disapprove of Trump's handling of the Iran war; Republican base approval remains elevated at 81% (up from 72% in March)

label: Iran War — Day 51: US Seizes Touska; Iran Refuses Islamabad Talks; Ceasefire in Doubt
source: Pentagon / Iranian & Lebanese health officials / Israeli authorities
contestNote: US Navy seized Iranian container ship Touska (294m) in Gulf of Oman Apr 20 — USS Spruance blew hole in engine room, Marines rappelled aboard; Iran called it 'piracy' and vowed retaliation once crew safety confirmed; Iran's Foreign Ministry refused to attend second Islamabad talks despite Vance/Witkoff/Kushner arriving in Pakistan; ceasefire expires Apr 22–23; Iran cites blockade and Touska seizure as US ceasefire violations
deltaNote: Day 51: Touska seized Apr 20; Iran refuses Islamabad talks; Vance delegation in Pakistan without Iranian counterpart; ceasefire expiry Apr 22–23 is critical deadline; gas prices avg $4.05 nationally; Energy Sec. Wright warns prices below $3 not until 2027

label: Brent Crude Oil Price (Apr 20, 2026 — Touska Seized; Talks Collapse)
deltaNote: Brent surged 4–6% after USS Spruance seized Iranian container ship Touska Apr 20 — compounding the Apr 19 Hormuz re-closure spike; Iran refused Islamabad talks, ceasefire expires Apr 22–23; US gas averaging $4.05 nationally (up from $2.98 pre-conflict); Energy Sec. Wright warns prices below $3/gallon not until 2027; IMF cut global growth forecast to 3.1% citing Iran war energy shock

label: "Big Beautiful Bill" Deficit Impact
contestNote: CBO projects $4.1T added deficit over 10 years; White House claims growth offsets costs; signed into law July 4, 2025
